query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## US SPR Crude Oil Level & Trajectory (EIA WCSSTUS1)

**Current level:** **319.489 million barrels** for the week ending **July 3, 2026** — the latest EIA reading and the **lowest SPR level since 1983**. Within June specifically, stocks fell from 349.2M (June 5) to 325.7M (June 26).

**Trajectory (past 3–6 months): sustained RELEASES, not fills**
- **Jan–mid-March 2026:** Flat at ~415 million barrels (pre-release plateau/peak of 415.4M).
- **Late March 2026 onward:** Accelerating releases began after a **March 11, 2026** authorization by the Trump administration/DOE to release **172 million barrels** — the largest single-country emergency release ever, part of a coordinated 400M-barrel IEA release by 32 nations, driven by the **Strait of Hormuz closure / war with Iran**.
- **April:** ~4–7M bbl/week (7.1M in week ending April 24 — biggest weekly draw since Oct 2022).
- **May:** Peak pace of ~8–10M bbl/week.
- **June–early July:** ~5.5–9M bbl/week.

**Cumulative:** ~95.9 million barrels drained from the mid-March peak to July 3 (~56% of the 172M program delivered), and down ~20.7% year-over-year.

Data corroborated across EIA's WCSSTUS1 historical table, EIA Today in Energy, DOE, Wikipedia, TradingEconomics, and YCharts. Note: the most recent official reading (July 3, 319.5M) is just past June; the closest strictly-June value is 325.7M (June 26).

sources.snippet: SPR crude dropped 5.5M barrels to 325.7M for week ending June 26, lowest since May 1983; part of a 172M-barrel release to offset shortages after the Iran war.

sources.title: US Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve Stocks
sources.snippet: 319.49M bbl for week of Jul 03 2026, down from 325.66M prior week and 402.76M one year ago (-20.68% YoY).
